original: RAF rear gunner Nicholas Alkemade survives a jump from his Lancaster bomber 18,000 feet over Germany without a parachute; his fall is broken by pine trees and soft snow, and he suffers only a sprained leg
NASA's Opportunity rover becomes the first to complete a Martian marathon
Space Shuttle STS-45 (Atlantis 11) launches into space with the first Belgian astronaut on board, Dirk Frimout
Tom Hunter swims world record 50m freestyle (21.81 sec)
NY Yankees beat NY Mets, 9-3
Michigan State'sEarvin"Magic" Johnson registers a triple-double with 29 points, 10 rebounds, and 10 assists as the Spartans cruise to a 101-67 victory over Penn State
"The Matrix" film written and directed by The Wachowskis, starring Keanu Reeve, Lawrence Fishburne and Carrie-Ann Moss premieres
Janet Harmon Bragg, American pilot (1st female black aviator to hold a commercial pilot's license), born in Griffin, Georgia (d. 1993)[1]
German scientistRobert Kochdiscovers and describes the tubercle bacillus, which causes tuberculosis (Mycobacterium tuberculosis), establishing germ theory
Mary Berry, English food writer and TV presenter (The Great British Bake Off), born in Bath, England
Fraser Stoddart, Scottish-American chemist, 2016 Nobel Prize for Chemistry for work developing molecular machines, born in Edinburgh, Scotland (d. 2024)[1]
Robert T. Bakker, American paleontologist known for revitalizing popular interest in dinosaurs (Dinosaur Renaissance), born in Ridgewood, New Jersey[1][2]
Ayrton Sennawins the Brazilian Grand Prix for the 1st time, at Interlagos, São Paulo
US Ranger 9 strikes Moon, 10 miles (16 km) NE of crater Alphonsus
Ub Iwerks, American animator and special-effects technician who worked on the design on Mickey Mouse, born in Kansas City, Missouri (d. 1971)[1][2]
Reinhard Genzel, German astrophysicist (2020 Nobel Prize for Physics for work on black holes), born in Bad Homburg vor der Höhe, West Germany
Nena [Gabrielle Kerner], German pop singer ("99 Luftballons"- "99 Red Balloons"), born in Hagen, West Germany
NASA publishes "Strategy for Safely Returning the Space Shuttle to Flight Status"
Norman Gifford makes cricket ODI debuts at age 44 (v Aust, Sharjah)
Doug Jarvis, Canadian ice hockey forward (record for most consecutive NHL games 964; Stanley Cup 1976-79 Montreal Canadiens), born in Brantford, Ontario
Columbia flown on carrier aircraft lands at Kennedy Space Center
Africa's first centralised biobank -  Biodiversity Biobanks South Africa, collecting more than a million biodiversity samples opens in Pretoria, South Africa
John Wesley Powell, American geologist, explorer (Grand Canyon) and ethnologist, born in Mount Morris, New York (d. 1902)
John Rock, American obstetrician and gynecologist who co-developed the 1st birth control pill, born in Marlborough, Massachusetts (d. 1984)
76 Allied officers escape Stalag Luft 3 (Great Escape)
Dutch soccer forward Huug de Groot scores twice as Netherlands score first ever victory over England; 2-1 at HBS, The Hague
Detroit Red Wings beat Montreal Maroons, 1-0 in 16:30 of 6th period of OT; record Stanley Cup playoff game lasts 9 periods (176 minutes)
Operation Varsity: In the largest one-day airborne operation of all time, British, US & Canadian paratroopers land east of the Rhine in Northern Germany
Great demonstrations against apartheid in South Africa
Anti-slavery newspaper "The Provincial Freeman" first published in Windsor, Ontario, edited by Samuel Ringgold Ward andMary Ann Shadd Cary, first black woman publisher in North America
Muhammad AliTKOs Chuck Wepner in 15 for heavyweight boxing title
NBC rebroadcastsMary Martinand Cyril Ritchard's 1960 version of "Peter Pan" for the first time since 1973
US version of "The Office" created byRicky Gervaisand Stephen Merchant, developed by Greg Daniels, starringSteve CarellandJohn Krasinskipremieres on NBC
Japan's Prime MinisterShinzō Abeannounces the postponement of the Tokyo 2020 Olympic and Paralympic Games to the summer of 2021 due to the global COVID-19 pandemic
Scottish KingJames VI, son of Mary, Queen of Scots, becomesKing James Iof England in succession to Elizabeth I, uniting the English and Scottish crowns
John Kendrew, British molecular biologist and winner of the Nobel Prize in Chemistry 1962, born in Oxford, England (d. 1997)[1]
Kenneth S. Reightler Jr. American astronaut (STS 48, 60), born in Patuxent River, Maryland
Yasser Seirawan, Syrian-born American chess grandmaster and four-time United States champion, born in Damascus, Syria
Canada grants its Black citizens the right to vote
Wings release single  "With a Little Luck"
South African President F.W. Willem de Klerk announces to South Africa's Parliament the existence and abandonment of the former nuclear weapons program
1st performance ofJean Sibelius' 7th Symphony in C at the Konsertföreningenin in Stockholm
Dmitri Shostakovich's Suite for Jazz Orchestra No. 1 premieres in Leningrad, USSR
New England Patriots tight endRob Gronkowskiannounces retirement as 3-time Super Bowl winner, tight end postseason record, most receptions (81), receiving yards (1,163) and receiving TDs (12)
Josef Stefan, Slovenian-Austrian physicist (Stefan-Boltzmann law), born in St. Peter, Austria (d. 1893)
Charlie Daniels, American swimmer (Olympic gold 220yd, 440yd, 4x50yd freestyle 1904, 100m freestyle 1908), born in Dayton, Ohio (d. 1973)
Dorothy Stratton, American coast guard officer (1st woman officer in US Coast Guard, SPARS), born in Brookfield, Missouri (d. 2006)
Lee Oskar [Levitin], Danish rock and soul harmonica player (War - "Why Can't We Be Friends?", "Low Rider"), born in Copenhagen, Denmark

Nick Lowe, British rock bassist, singer, songwriter (Brinsley Schwarz - "What's So Funny About Peace, Love, and Understanding"; Rockpile - "Teacher, Teacher"; solo - "Cruel To Be Kind"), and producer (Elvis Costello; Graham Parker), born in Walton-on-Thames, England
Scott J Horowitz, PhD/Capt USAF/astronaut (STS 75, 82), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Edmond Becquerel, French physicist who studied the solar spectrum, magnetism, electricity and optics (photovoltaic effect), born in Paris, France (d. 1891)
Clipper Andrew Jackson arrives in San Francisco, 89 days out of New York
1st telephone call between NY & Chicago
New York City Mayor Robert Anderson Van Wyck breaks ground for a new underground "Rapid Transit Railroad" that would link Manhattan and Brooklyn.
83°F highest temperature ever recorded in Cleveland in March
Bombay beat Delhi by innings & 46 to win Ranji Cricket Trophy
IOC agrees to 6-team exhibition baseball tournament in Olympics
Bhutan officially becomes a democracy, with its first ever general election.
China's Hubei province, the original center of the COVID-19 outbreak, eases restrictions on travel after a nearly two-month lockdown
Glenn Millerbegins work on his 1st movie "Sun Valley Serenade", starringSonja Henieand John Payne, for 20th Century Fox
